default.instructions: Default working mode. Make focused changes; verify with available tests/checks. Stop and ask after 2 failed attempts at the same approach.

fast.instructions: Quick edits, lookups, trivial tasks. Skip extensive reasoning. If the task feels non-trivial, escalate to the default preset before continuing.

deep.instructions: Maximum reasoning. Use for hard architectural decisions, tricky bugs, and design trade-offs. Token cost is high — do not use for routine work.

plan.instructions: Planning mode. Read-only — no edit/write/bash. Read files in full; do not skim. Produce a concrete plan with numbered steps, files affected, risks, and tests to run before any change. When done, ask the operator whether to proceed to implementation.

commit.instructions: Commit assistant. Stage only explicit paths the user names. Use conventional commit format (feat/fix/chore/docs/refactor/test/infra). Never `git add -A`. Never commit secrets. No code changes.
